desc: finally did a skincare routine 🦦💗 this is just what i use and it might not work for everyone! i found the best way to treat my acne and other skin issues was learning more about my skin (my skin type, what kind of acne with my dermatologist) and my biggest piece of advice for anyone who is dealing with skin issues: it’s not permanent and things take time! a pimple can take 1-2 weeks to form and a few more to go away and a lot of products take just as long to show results. i learned to normalize acne/skin issues because everyone goes through them and that’s helped my anxiety/stress when it comes to how my skin looks so much. i’ll probably make another video about how i desensitized myself to my skin issues and how important that is 🤍 i just started up tretinoin a month ago at 0.025% twice a week and my skin has been incredibly dry but i think i’ve finished most of the purging 🙂 excited to see more results from it! products 🪽: @mixsoon_official bean cleansing oil + centella cleansing foam @anua.skincare_us toner pads @mediheal_official tea tree toner pads @Pyunkang Yul (편강율) essence toner @imfrom_global mugwort essence @apieu.official madecassoside cream 2x tretinoin 0.025% (prescription) @Kiehl’s Since 1851 avocado eye cream @Laniege_us lip sleeping mask #skincare #skincareroutine #skincareproducts #koreanskincare #asianskincare #koreanskincareroutine #koreanskin #glassskin #acnetreatment

desc: Replying to @genn.sui Full Face of drugstore makeup? THOUGHTS👀 ________________ LETS TALK BABIES🫶🏽 I’ve found that my audience of ppl who prefer affordable makeup and reviews have faded, as I’ve been focusing on my artistry for the last couple years. That being said, this was long overdue! These drugstore items are still gorgeous, although I do prefer most of my higher end! I find that they give me a longer hold than some of these items, but that’s why my routine consists of a little bit of both🫶🏽 BUT this video does show that you don’t have to break the bank for a gorgeous finish, just need the right application 🗣  BREAK DOWN⤵️ Power Grip Primer @elfcosmetics - This is veryyy sticky and bouncy! Idk how it would do underneath every formula of foundation, but it felt great!  Super stay Foundation “Toffee” @maybelline - THE OG! I love the finish of this super matte formula! The shade match is also great… definitely not for our dry girlies🥲  16hr Camo Concealer “Deep Caramel” & Tan Sand” @elfcosmetics - 10/10 would recommend! Great beginner concealer and affordable! Gives nice blendable coverage and super light weight to build a great finish! 🔥 Matte Fit Me Foundation (as contour) “356 Warm Coconut” @maybelline - love this foundation as contour because it blends into the skin beautifully  Loose Face Powder “Naturally Neutral” @airspunofficial - ehhhh for how affordable it is, it gets the job done with no flashback for us melanin folks… BUT the fragrance is insane! Sensitive skin ppl- RUN! Stay All Day @elfcosmetics - I meannn my makeup indeed did NOT stay all day buttt it held pretty well😮‍💨 This sprayer is a 6/10 wish more came out at a time and more like mist. Lash Paradise Mascara @lorealparis - 8/10 definitely a nice mascara! I wish it was a darker black buttt still had my lower lashes nice and full after two coats🥰 (Couldn’t find a drugstore blush- need to vista Walmart next time!) Blush “PomPom” @benefitcosmetics  Blend Away Puff @beatsbydebcosmetics  Lashes @beatsbydebcosmetics  Snatch The Brow Pencil “Dark Brown” @beatsbydebcosmetics  LIP COMBO COMING!

desc: These are my top six non-toxic brands that honestly prove clean can still be so chic.  I think for a long time we were made to believe that choosing better ingredients meant giving something up… less performance, less fun, less aesthetic.  But that’s just not true anymore. What we put on our skin, what we spray on our bodies, even what we wear all day actually matters, and being aware of that doesn’t mean you stop enjoying beauty. It just means you start choosing things that work with your body instead of against it.  Looking good shouldn’t come with a hidden cost, and once you realize that, clean stops feeling like a downgrade and starts feeling like an upgrade. 🫰🏽🪽✨  #holisticliving #nontoxiclifestyle #cleanbeautyproducts #ugccreator

desc: My top tips to repair your skin barrier👇 1. Completely stop using actives eg retinoids, exfoliants, vitamin c until your barrier is healed - even if you are using a retinoid for acne you should stop until your barrier is healed because a damaged barrier can cause breakouts and you need a healthy barrier first to effectively treat acne 2. Stick to a simple routine including a gentle cleanser - don’t use any harsh cleansers, scrubs or peels these can further damage your barrier 3. Use a barrier repair cream until your skin is no longer tight, flaky, dry, peeling or stinging (see my post from 22nd Nov for best barrier creams and 25th Nov for best for acne) 4. If barrier creams break you out use lighter barrier repair products like toners and serums instead 5. Wait till your barrier is healed (this can take 2-6 weeks) before introducing exfoliants and retinoids again and when you do be super cautious (start with only x1 product 2x per week and increase as tolerated - it’s also best to start with a gentle active like PHA or a low % retinal). A big cause of barrier damage is using actives that are too strong for your skin, using them daily or even using multiple actives at once when you don’t have the tolerance (and maybe never will) so you need to start low and slow to avoid damage and see what you tolerate Let me know if you have any questions in the comments ❤️ #damagedskinbarrier #skinbarrierrepair #skintok #barrierrepair #acne

desc: This serum feels luxurious on the skin. The packaging is sleek and dispenses just the right amount of product. desc: ✅Barrier repair moisturizer products Ranking  by a Korean pharmacist 🇰🇷💊 ✔️Anua PDRN Serum - 5 PDRN sounds fancy, but it’s only 0.01%. Hydrating thanks to hyaluronic acid, but honestly… nothing special for the price. ✔️Menokin Bubble Mask Moist - 6 I debated this one, but too many reviews say the pump is messy. So… last place. ✔️Bepanthen cream - 4 Great for skin recovery with dexpanthenol, but very thick, and not originally made as a cosmetic. So it has clear limitations. ✔️La Roche-Posay Cicaplast B5 - 2 Amazing for very dry skin. But the shea butter makes it quite heavy— so not for everyone. ✔️Torriden Hyaluronic Acid Serum - 3 Very hydrating with a nice texture, but lacks barrier-repair ingredients. So not enough on its own. ✔️Aestura Atobarrier 365 Cream - 1 Ceramide + cholesterol + fatty acids base. Includes long-chain ceramides. Balanced texture, easy to use. This is the real barrier repair cream. #koreanskincare  #damagedskinbarrier  #skinbarrier  #dehydratedskin  #kbeauty  Which one is your favorite moisturizer?

desc: Once my skin barrier started healing ❤️‍🩹 I started using more than just warm water and moisturizer - funny enough this was sent to me during my time of recovery (still recovering)… Well I must say- I see the hype on milky toners! desc: CeraVe and similar skincare products are dermatologist-recommended, but are often TOXIC to your skin and health! Why is that? 👉 Simply put, “Dermatologist recommended” doesn’t mean safe or non-toxic! Here are the facts:  ❌ Over 50% of the ingredients in CeraVe are petroleum-based, many of which are hormone disruptors and can lead to more severe health issues over time, as well as end up irritating your skin instead of moisturizing it! Some of the more problematic ingredients include:  🧴 Ceramide (a known insulin and metabolism disruptor) 🧴 Petrolatum (a petroleum jelly refined oil) 🧴 Dimethicone (a silicone-based compound that locks in dirt and toxins on your face) None of these nourish or moisturize your skin…and if they end up in your body, they will only hinder your health! Here’s the bottom line: Stop slathering synthetic skincare lotions all over your skin - whether or not they are “dermatologist-recommended,” if you can’t eat it, don’t put it on your body!  ✅ For clean and non-toxic skincare products, ditch CeraVe and similar synthetic products and look for natural options like 100% organic shea butter, 100% organic coconut oil, or even 100% organic grass-fed, grass-finished beef tallow. #CeraVe #Skincare #SkincareTips #HealthySkin #NonToxicLiving

desc: Extremely dry, rough, or leathery skin can sometimes be more than a skincare issue, it can be a sign of an underlying health problem. Conditions like diabetes, kidney disease, and liver issues can all show changes on the skin. If you notice persistent dryness or texture changes that don’t improve with moisturizers, make sure to see both your primary care physician and a board-certified dermatologist. Early evaluation can help identify the cause and guide proper treatment. Original video: @Deniel Foot and Ankle Center  #boardcertifieddermatologist #dryskin #dmv

desc: Get the most from your skincare with this simple trick! Skin is more absorbent when it's slightly damp so skincare will penetrate better on skin that's not completely dry! I lightly pat my skin after washing and immediately apply my skincare with no waiting between steps for maximum absorption of my active ingredients! When first starting with ingredients that can be irritating (retinoids, vitamin c, AHAs) it's best to apply to dry skin at first to minimize the chance of irritation. When skin acclimates to the ingredient you can start applying to damp skin! #Skincaretips #skincare #antiagingskincare

desc: Been evaluating the new @Rare Beauty soft pinch liquid contours and here’s the tea beauty queens. Is this a contour? In the traditional artistry / editorial sense, no. Does that mean it’s a bad product and shouldn’t be considered a contour? No. It can be used as a contour. IMHO Rare Beauty is not an artistry brand, and at its core ethos is accessible easy to use and love products. What Rare Beauty strived to do here - from my perspective - is provide a versatile easy to use for any level of user sculpting product. Personally, I would call these hybrid contour bronzers (omg BRONTOURS 🤣 sounds like a dinosaur though 🦖…). But ultimately the choice is yours beauty queens. What do you think? desc: I grew up playing in my grandmas drawers of overflowing creams and serums, maybe that’s where my skincare obsession started. Speaking of her, she would be rolling in her grave watching me miss my décolletage at almost 40.  Anyways, the difference of her collection vs mine is only about 500,000 different chemicals. Clean skincare is something I will splurge on because its something I put on my largest organ twice a day. I’ve tried them ALL. I liked certain things about certain lines, LOVED the Herbal Face Food for my melasma, and then my skin adapted and it just felt greasy. LOVED the Oliveda for a while, but only certain ones and def. didn’t see the results from it that are all over social. So I tried something new and I want to share because not only is the price right, the ingredients are BEYOND anything I’ve tried, down to the promise of how this is manufactured (which yes, that is where things are exposed to a lot of carcinogens, its that important!).  These products will never have dyes, fragrance, parabens, silicones, or ANY endocrine disruptors for that matter. And, I love the way my skin feels. Comment LOWTOXSKIN if you’re looking for some new non tox products you can trust!
